content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the right ahead with the dashed center line visible curving right, and the car appears well-positioned on the track heading into the curve.

Target waypoint: nearest_waypoints[1] + 5 = 19 + 5 = 24
Waypoint 24: (6.643739, 0.768311)
Car position: (5.852503, 0.651630)
Bearing = atan2(0.768311 - 0.651630, 6.643739 - 5.852503) = atan2(0.116681, 0.791236) = atan2(0.1474) ≈ 8.39°
Current heading: -1.263°
Delta = 8.39 - (-1.263) = 9.65° → slight left needed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves right ahead as visible in image; delta to waypoint 24 is +9.65° (left), so apply 5° left steering to smoothly track the waypoint line into the approaching right curve.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track starts with a long straight section heading in +X direction along y≈0.683",
    "Large right-hand curve begins around waypoint 24-30 (y increases significantly)",
    "Car is nearly centered on the straight, delta near 5-10° as curve approaches",
    "Use steering_angle=2.5 for delta 5-8°, steering_angle=5 for delta 8-12° to avoid oscillation",
    "After the right curve, track loops back left and returns — full circuit",
    "Speed 1.5 is max and appropriate for straight sections and gentle curves",
    "Waypoints 0-23 are nearly perfectly straight along y≈0.683",
    "Right curve starts gently at wp24 (y increases), sharpens at wp27-30",
    "Car heading stays near -1.2° on the straight — minimal correction needed",
    "Delta ~9.65° at wp24 suggests beginning to track into the right curve now"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [5.5344, 5.6502, 5.7336, 5.8525],
    "y": [0.6577, 0.6556, 0.6541, 0.6516],
    "yaw": [-1.112, -1.131, -1.165, -1.263]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 9.65,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 5.0
}
```
